Identity.History: Camels were used in ancient Near Eastern warfare mainly by desert and frontier peoples, including Arabs, Nabataeans, Persians, and others. They served as mounts, pack animals, and mobile platforms for archers or javelin throwers, especially in arid regions where horses struggled. Camel troops were valuable for raiding, scouting, and desert logistics, carrying supplies across long distances with little water. Their smell and unfamiliar movement could also unsettle horses. Though usually less decisive than cavalry, camels gave armies strategic mobility in deserts and steppe environments.
